game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Super Special Attack
special_mechanics.description: Charged special attack performed by filling a green Mutagen Meter under the life bar through hitting opponents, then pressing both strong attack buttons simultaneously

special_mechanics.name: Power-up System
special_mechanics.description: Flying monitor with Splinter's face occasionally appears during matches, dropping a red ball power-up at stage center that either fighter can retrieve and activate via command input

special_mechanics.name: Game Speed Adjustment
special_mechanics.description: Option to enhance fight speed, making matches more intense but more difficult to follow

tier_notes: Tier 2 (empirical/qualitative) - This is a fighting game with focus on combat mechanics rather than movement physics. Available sources document game modes, characters, control schemes, and regional differences, but contain no quantitative movement parameters, gravity values, or sub-pixel system data. The game features four-button fighting controls (weak/strong punch and kick) with a super special attack meter mechanic, but specific movement physics are undocumented.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - TMNT Tournament Fighters SNES article
Wikipedia - Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters article
